storyline:
Rheinmetall (RNMBY) is Europe's largest ammunition + armored-vehicle maker - the frontline beneficiary of the European rearmament wave. The catalyst: Germany's March-2025 vote to exempt defense from its constitutional debt brake, a datable policy shock that unlocked a historic multi-year military buildout and sent Rheinmetall's order book and shares soaring. As Europe scrambles to refill depleted stockpiles and stand up its own defense-industrial base, Rheinmetall - the continent's dominant maker of shells, artillery and armored vehicles - is the purest way to own the buildout. This dashboard tracks the rearmament event as agent ev3 (the thick green curve in the plots below). Exit condition: the rearmament thesis is open-ended, with no single resolution date, so the position exits only on a genuine reversal - the debt-brake exemption repealed, or the rearmament rationale undone by a Ukraine peace deal / ceasefire that ends the defense super-cycle. In this scan that reversal never came - RNMBY held to the scan end and never exited (an open-ended structural driver, the MP-type under-exit case).
This gem’s event-agent (ev3) — catalyst: Germany exempts defense from debt brake, funds rearmament. Exit: still live — none - debt-brake exemption remains in force, no repeal or reversal of German rearmament rationale found.
Co-discovered events the curator juggled (not this dashboard's gem):
  • defensive · GLD — catalyst: always-on defensive (GLD) floor agent. Exit: still live (catalyst active).
  • ev1 · CCJ, DNN, OKLO — catalyst: Amazon's SMR/nuclear investment deal lifts uranium miners. Exit: still live — none - no hyperscaler nuclear retreat, SMR cancellation, or export-curb reversal has ever appeared.
  • ev2 · MP, UAMY, USAR — catalyst: China bans antimony/gallium/germanium exports to US. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2025-10-31 — Trump-Xi summit produced trade truce; China suspended rare-earth/critical-mineral export controls for a year, reversing the curb.
  • ev4 · RKLB — catalyst: Rocket Lab selected to bid on US/UK launch contracts. Exit: still live — none - no award, denial, or cancellation reported for either US or UK program; driving condition unchanged.
  • ev5 · SATS — catalyst: EchoStar sold spectrum licenses to SpaceX for $17B. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2025-09-12 — Deal announced/signed 2025-09-08-09; spectrum sale to SpaceX is already public and priced.
  • ev6 · VLO — catalyst: California weighs paying Valero to avert Benicia refinery closure. Exit: still live — Twelve+ weeks total silence on an acute negotiation suggests it stalled or was quietly deprioritized, not that it reversed.

seeds.title: Rheinmetall, still under-owned, is the frontline winner as Germany exempts defence from its debt brake
seeds.snippet: Germany's Bundestag voted on March 18 to exempt defence spending from the constitutional debt brake, kicking off a historic multi-year military buildout. Rheinmetall, Europe's largest ammunition and armored-vehicle maker, is the purest and most direct beneficiary - and despite the move it remains under-owned, with the spending surge only just beginning.

seeds.title: European defence stocks fall on signs of U.S. push over Ukraine war
seeds.snippet: European aerospace and defence stocks fell more than 3% on Wednesday on signs of a fresh U.S.-led push to end the Russia-Ukraine war, which also lifted Ukraine's government bonds. Shares in Rheinmetall, Renk, BAE Systems, Leonardo, Saab were among the biggest fallers, with losses ranging from 4% to 7%. Two traders pointed to a Politico report about a possible framework agreement over Ukraine as a trigger for the drop.

CCJ.thesis: Amazon's SMR/nuclear investment deal lifts uranium miners
CCJ.exit_case: none
CCJ.assessment: Amazon's SMR investment is the latest in a string of hyperscaler nuclear commitments (following Microsoft/Constellation, Google/Kairos) confirming structural demand for uranium and SMR technology. Coverage is still framed as a fresh catalyst (Benzinga/Yahoo just flagging the move), not yet fully mainstream-saturated, and no concrete counter-event (curbs lifted, deal cancelled) exists. Uranium ETFs and pure-plays (CCJ, DNN, UUUU, OKLO) all rallied on the news, confirming basket-wide relevance. Treat this as a structural driver (tech-sector nuclear procurement wave) with likely follow-on catalysts (more hyperscaler deals, SMR licensing milestones) rather than a single datable event.
CCJ.exit_advice: Hold — this is the entry week; the Amazon SMR/nuclear deal just broke and is driving a basket-wide uranium/nuclear rally. No reversal or resolution of the underlying driver (accelerating tech-sector nuclear procurement) is visible; this is a structural theme (hyperscaler nuclear demand) likely to generate further milestones (Google, Microsoft, Meta deals; SMR approvals). Stay in the basket.

CCJ.thesis: Amazon's SMR/nuclear investment deal lifts uranium miners
CCJ.exit_case: none - no reversal of hyperscaler nuclear demand; Amazon deal stands, no curbs or cancellations
CCJ.assessment: News this week (etftrends, biztoc x2) confirms uranium miner rally continuing and X-energy CEO calling Amazon deal a 'game changer' - reinforcing rather than resolving the structural thesis. Coverage is heavier (multiple outlets, ETF-level framing) but not yet at full mainstream-saturation with no concrete next step; more hyperscaler deals and SMR regulatory/licensing catalysts remain plausible and unannounced-but-expected. This is a structural driver (tech-sector nuclear procurement wave), not a single datable event, so per structural rules we hold through the current quiet/rally stretch and only exit on a genuine reversal (curbs, deal cancellations, hyperscalers pulling back).
CCJ.exit_advice: hold basket; structural hyperscaler nuclear procurement wave (Amazon/Microsoft/Google) continues with likely follow-on deals and SMR licensing milestones ahead - no concrete counter-event has occurred

CCJ.thesis: Amazon's SMR/nuclear investment deal lifts uranium miners
